description_long: This object is a large, vintage IBM 3350 disk storage unit, characterized by its boxy, dual-cabinet design. The front features two prominent rectangular black access panels set into a light gray top section, each with a small, colorful control panel above. The lower half of the unit is finished in a distinctive blue, with a black vertical divider and black side panels. A yellow number '5' and the IBM 3350 label are visible on the right cabinet, indicating its model and likely its position in a series. The top surfaces are flat and slightly raised at the front, while the sides and back are plain and solid. This robust machine would have been a common sight in data centers or technical facilities, and its size and construction suggest it is both heavy and durable.
description: A large, dual-cabinet IBM 3350 disk storage unit with blue and black panels, gray tops, and colorful control panels. The front features model labeling and prominent access panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing two black access panels, colorful control panels, blue lower panels, and the IBM 3350 label with a yellow number '5'.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the solid black side panels and the protruding gray tops of the cabinets.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain blue and black panels with no visible controls or labels.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with solid black side panels and the gray tops visible.
attribute_brand_logo: A small IBM logo is visible on the front right cabinet, above the blue panel and next to the model number '3350'. The logo is white on a black background, positioned near a yellow number '5'.
attribute_control_panel_design: Two small rectangular control panels are located on the top front of each cabinet, each featuring a set of colored square buttons and a small display area.
